tools.name: GMAIL_CREATE_EMAIL_DRAFT
tools.description: Creates a Gmail email draft. All fields are optional per the Gmail API - drafts can be created with minimal content and edited later before sending. Supports To/Cc/Bcc recipients, subject, plain/HTML body (ensure `is_html=True` for HTML), attachments, and threading. When creating a draft reply to an existing thread (thread_id provided), leave subject empty to stay in the same thread; setting a subject will create a NEW thread instead.

tools.name: GMAIL_DELETE_DRAFT
tools.description: Permanently deletes a specific Gmail draft using its ID; ensure the draft exists and the user has necessary permissions for the given `user_id`.

tools.name: GMAIL_DELETE_MESSAGE
tools.description: Permanently deletes a specific email message by its ID from a Gmail mailbox; for `user_id`, use 'me' for the authenticated user or an email address to which the authenticated user has delegated access.

tools.name: GMAIL_FETCH_EMAILS
tools.description: Fetches a list of email messages from a Gmail account, supporting filtering, pagination, and optional full content retrieval.

tools.name: GMAIL_FETCH_MESSAGE_BY_MESSAGE_ID
tools.description: Fetches a specific email message by its ID, provided the `message_id` exists and is accessible to the authenticated `user_id`.

tools.name: GMAIL_GET_ATTACHMENT
tools.description: Retrieves a specific attachment by ID from a message in a user's Gmail mailbox, requiring valid message and attachment IDs.

tools.name: GMAIL_LIST_DRAFTS
tools.description: Retrieves a paginated list of email drafts from a user's Gmail account. Use verbose=true to get full draft details including subject, body, sender, and timestamp.

tools.name: GMAIL_MOVE_TO_TRASH
tools.description: Moves an existing, non-deleted email message to the trash for the specified user.

tools.name: GMAIL_PATCH_LABEL
tools.description: Patches the specified label.

tools.name: GMAIL_REPLY_TO_THREAD
tools.description: Sends a reply within a specific Gmail thread using the original thread's subject, requiring a valid `thread_id` and at least one of recipient_email, cc, or bcc must be provided. Supports attachments via the `attachment` parameter with valid `s3key`, `mimetype`, and `name`.

tools.name: GMAIL_SEARCH_PEOPLE
tools.description: Searches contacts by matching the query against names, nicknames, emails, phone numbers, and organizations, optionally including 'Other Contacts'.

tools.name: GMAIL_SEND_DRAFT
tools.description: Sends an existing draft email AS-IS to recipients already defined within the draft. IMPORTANT: This action does NOT accept recipient parameters (to, cc, bcc). The Gmail API's drafts/send endpoint sends drafts to whatever recipients are already set in the draft's To, Cc, and Bcc headers - it cannot add or override recipients. If the draft has no recipients, you must either: 1. Create a new draft with recipients using GMAIL_CREATE_EMAIL_DRAFT, then send it 2. Use GMAIL_SEND_EMAIL to send a new email directly with recipients This action only requires the draft_id parameter.

tools.name: GMAIL_SEND_EMAIL
tools.description: Sends an email via Gmail API using the authenticated user's Google profile display name. At least one of 'to' (or 'recipient_email'), 'cc', or 'bcc' must be provided. At least one of subject or body must be provided. Requires `is_html=True` if the body contains HTML. For attachments, you must provide a FileUploadable object with valid `s3key` (obtained from a previous file download action like GMAIL_GET_ATTACHMENT), `mimetype` (e.g., 'image/png', 'application/pdf'), and `name` (filename). All common file types including PNG, JPG, PDF, MP4, etc. are supported. Gmail API limits total message size to ~25 MB after base64 encoding.

tools.name: reply_to_user
tools.description: Sends a message to the end user.

tools.name: reply_and_await_response
tools.description: Sends a message to the end user and awaits for the response. Useful to ask data to the user using the txt as a lable

tools.name: close_conversation
tools.description: Closes the conversation

tools.name: woocommerce-products-list
tools.description: Retrieve a paginated list of products with optional filters for status, category, price range, and other attributes.

tools.name: woocommerce-products-get
tools.description: Retrieve detailed information about a single product by ID, including price, description, images, and metadata.

tools.name: woocommerce-products-create
tools.description: Create a new product in WooCommerce with name, price, description, and other product attributes.

tools.name: woocommerce-products-update
tools.description: Update an existing product by modifying its attributes such as price, stock, description, or metadata.

tools.name: woocommerce-products-delete
tools.description: Permanently delete a product from the store. This action cannot be undone.

tools.name: woocommerce-orders-list
tools.description: Retrieve a paginated list of orders with optional filters for status, customer, date range, and other criteria.

tools.name: woocommerce-orders-get
tools.description: Retrieve detailed information about a single order by ID, including line items, customer details, and payment information.

tools.name: woocommerce-orders-create
tools.description: Create a new order with customer information, line items, shipping details, and payment information.

tools.name: woocommerce-orders-update
tools.description: Update an existing order by modifying status, customer information, line items, or other order details.
